key_finding: The two lanes CONVERGE on one substrate: the single fused OWNED AMDGCN attention tile. (a) It is the untested bounded attention lever (B4/B5 only ever used SEPARATE tile->combine; folding the combine in removes the part/meta HBM round-trip the +5.7% ceiling never tested). (b) It is ALSO the OPAQUE attention read Lane 1 needs to remove the KV copy -- the default functional gqa_coop_vec reduce re-hits the read-after-write hazard, an opaque tile does not. Lane 1 CANNOT go first (it requires this tile).

ranking.lane: Single fused owned AMDGCN tile (Lane 2) -- the convergence substrate
ranking.expected_gain: attention up to +7%@4096 (UNCERTAIN) AND, by serving as the opaque cache read, unlocks KV-copy removal ~+1.4ms/+8tok/s@1024 (MEASURED transfer)
ranking.why_now: clearest bounded first gate; captures the MEASURED copy-removal win as a fallback even if attention rests; tests the one untried attention lever

ranking.expected_gain: +1.4ms/+8tok/s@1024 (measured), flat across ctx (bigger relative at short/medium ctx)
ranking.confidence: high transfer, but BLOCKED on the Lane-2 opaque tile
ranking.impl_size: medium-high (runtime KV object + lifecycle + opaque append, which exists)
ranking.why_not_first: requires the opaque attention read (the Lane-2 tile); becomes a fast follow-on once the tile lands

ranking.lane: Native linearizer / renderer fused-flash (Lane 2 routes A/C)
ranking.impl_size: large, CORE tinygrad, multi-week, all-backend blast radius
ranking.why_not: UNBOUNDED; fund only AFTER the escape-hatch single tile proves the ceiling is worth it; doc evidence shows linearizer expressiveness isn't even the blocker (codegen quality is)

ranking.why_now: independent of both lanes; do in parallel regardless

ranking_rule_application.rule1_clearest_gate_highest_transfer: the single fused tile has the clearest BOUNDED gate; its copy-removal-unlock is MEASURED-transfer, its attention gain is uncertain
ranking_rule_application.rule2_lower_blast_radius: both fork-local; the tile is the prerequisite
ranking_rule_application.rule3_no_theoretical_over_measured: the recommendation rests on the MEASURED copy-removal transfer (which the tile unlocks), not the theoretical attention gain
ranking_rule_application.rule4_both_unbounded_promote_gemv: NOT triggered -- the single tile is bounded; but promote GEMV in PARALLEL anyway

decision_detail: Fund the single fused owned AMDGCN tile FIRST as the gate-first, fork-local convergence substrate. It (1) tests the one untried bounded attention lever and (2) is the opaque cache read that unlocks the measured KV-copy removal (Lane 1, as the immediate follow-on). HARD STOP: if the single fused tile still saturates <=+5.7%@4096 in W==D, attention is structurally overlap-bounded -> REST_DECODE, but STILL pair it with the opaque KV append to capture the measured +1.4ms copy removal. Promote Q4K_GEMV_WARP to default (owner decision) in PARALLEL -- it is already won and independent. Do NOT fund the native-linearizer/renderer routes now (unbounded core tinygrad); revisit only if the escape-hatch tile proves a >+7% attention ceiling worth the blast radius.
first_gate: single fused owned tile: one AMDGCN graph node (combine folded into owned_flash_tile_gqa, no part/meta round-trip), default-off, byte-correct vs gqa_coop_vec/numpy, local A/B >=1.5x vs gqa_coop_vec @ctx1024, W==D projection before in-model route; stop if local gate misses.
